notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 2, Book 8 source file.
Developing card: moderate length with more vocabulary and plot detail.

book.title: The Tale of Tom Kitten
book.description: The Tale of Tom Kitten includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: moderate length with more vocabulary and plot detail

weeks.title: The Tale of Tom Kitten Book Card
reading: Chapter 2: Elegant Clothes for Little Kittens
Chapter 4: The Clothes Begin to Come Apart
vocabulary: 1. particular - very careful and exact about details; wanting everything to be just right
2. lengthy - taking a long time to complete
1. sophisticated - elegant and fashionable; very fancy
2. considerably - by a large amount; much more
1. restrictive - limiting movement; too tight to move freely
2. sedately - in a calm, slow, and dignified manner
1. catastrophe - a sudden disaster; something that goes very wrong
2. tatters - torn pieces; badly ripped fabric
1. disheveled - messy and untidy; not neat in appearance
2. distinguished - having an elegant and important appearance
1. sympathetic - understanding and caring about someone's problems
2. parading - walking around proudly to show off
journal: Write from Tom Kitten’s point of view after he gets into trouble.
Explain why listening to directions matters in this story.
writing: Write from Tom Kitten’s point of view after he gets into trouble.
Explain why listening to directions matters in this story.
questions: 1. What were the names of the three kittens?
2. Why was Mrs. Tabitha preparing the kittens?
1. What color were the dresses that Mittens and Moppet wore?
2. What was the problem with Tom's clothes?
1. What did Mrs. Tabitha tell the kittens NOT to do?
2. How was Tom feeling in his tight clothes?
1. What happened first to Tom's clothes?
2. How did Tom feel when his clothes were ruined?
1. Who appeared in the garden to help Tom?
2. What did the ducks do with Tom's clothes?
1. What was Tom's punishment for losing his clothes?
2. How did Tom feel about not wearing the tight clothes anymore?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
